heading: Assistant Attorney General for Administration
label: 507 Assistant Attorney General for Administration
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large [Uncodified].1950 Reorg. Plan No. 2, § 5 eff. , .May 24, 195064 Stat. 1261The title of the position was changed to “Assistant Attorney General for Administration” by , , .§ 307 of the Act of Aug. 14, 1964Pub. L. 88–42678 Stat. 432The words “competitive service” are substituted for “classified civil service” because the term “classified civil service” formerly used to designate the merit system established by the Civil Service Act of 1883 has become ambiguous due to the creation of the “classified” pay system. The term “competitive service” is now customarily used, and appears throughout title 5, United States Code, in place of “classified civil service”.The words “There shall be in the Department of Justice” are omitted as unnecessary as the title of the position and the fact of appointment by the Attorney General establish the location of the position in the Department of Justice.The last 12 words of section 5 of the Reorganization Plan are omitted on authority of the (3d proviso), . The salary of the position is now fixed by , , , which is codified in , United States Code.Act of June 5, 1952, ch. 369, § 110166 Stat. 121§ 303(e) of the Act of Aug. 14, 1964Pub. L. 88–42678 Stat. 420section 5316 of title 5

L. 89–554, § 8(a)section 4(c) of Pub. L. 89–554

children.label: a
children.text: The Attorney General shall appoint, with the approval of the President, an Assistant Attorney General for Administration, who shall perform such duties as the Attorney General may prescribe.

children.label: b
children.text: The position of Assistant Attorney General for Administration is in the competitive service.

children.label: c
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of , United States Code, the Assistant Attorney General for Administration shall be the Chief Financial Officer of the Department of Justice.section 901 of title 31
